etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "wood") + phonetic 攸(攸) (yōu) -> originally indicates a small branch or wooden stick
etymology.interpretation: The character 條(條) (tiáo, "strip; streak; item") combines the semantic 木(木) (mù, "wood") to suggest something made of wood, and the phonetic 攸(攸) (yōu) to provide the pronunciation. Initially, it referred specifically to a small branch or stick made of wood, which logically leads to the modern extended meanings of a 'strip' or 'item' in more general contexts because of the similar shape and form.
etymology.simplification: 条(條) simplifies 條 by removing the 木(木) (mù, "wood") component and simplifying 攸(攸) (yōu) to a simpler form, focusing on phonetics rather than semantic resemblance to wood.

examples.chinese: 这条裙子很好看(這條裙子很好看)。
河上架了一座长长的桥(河上架了一座長長的橋)。
examples.pinyin: Hé shàng jiàle yī zuò cháng cháng de qiáo.
examples.english: A long bridge was built over the river.

examples.chinese: 他发送了一条短信(他發送了一條短信)，通知朋友聚会时间(通知朋友聚會時間)。
examples.pinyin: Tā fāsòngle yī tiáo duǎnxìn, tōngzhī péngyǒu jùhuì shíjiān.
examples.english: He sent a text message, informing friends of the gathering time.
